title: epipactis purpurata subsp. kuenkeleana
intro: Epipactis purpurata subsp. kuenkeleana, also known as the Kuenkele's helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit bacterial growth, making it effective against infections and inflammation. The health benefits of Epipactis purpurata subsp. kuenkeleana include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or tincture, and its herbal preparations are used in folk medicine to address various health concerns, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
